docs.title: 🥒 DLL Pickle
docs.text: A PowerShell module that helps you get un-stuck from dependency version conflicts that often occur when trying to connect to multiple Microsoft services.

docs.title: 📝 Description
docs.text:
Let’s start with a few FAQs:

  • What does DLL Pickle actually do?

DLL Pickle pre-loads the newest version of DLLs that may be required by your other installed modules and scripts. (See the example below.)

  • Why does it need to do this?

It is common for PowerShell modules to ship with DLLs that provide precompiled functionality or dependencies. A PowerShell session cannot import two different versions of the same library (DLL). If a module imports one version of a DLL and then a different module attempts to import a second, newer version of that DLL, they will typically see an error that says, “an assembly with the same name is already loaded.”

  • Why don’t modules use Application Load Context (ALC) to solve this problem?

ALC can be complex to implement and is only available in PowerShell 7, so it is not commonly used in public modules.

  • Do these issues look familiar?
Related GitHub Issues - [Connect-MgGraph fails after Connect-ExchangeOnline](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-powershell/issues/3394) - [UUF Customer feedback: Get-EntraExtensionProperty error](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/entra-powershell/issues/1258) (Assembly with same name is already loaded) - [Assembly with same name is already loaded](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/entra-powershell/issues/1083) - [Design entra-powershell with ALC bridge pattern for a more robust assembly load handling](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/entra-powershell/issues/1242) - [Implement proper assembly isolation to play nice with vscode-powershell](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-powershell/issues/2978) - [Conflict with the .Net dll of the Az module PowerShell/vscode-powershell#3012](https://github.com/PowerShell/vscode-powershell/issues/3012) - [Unable to load Az modules - Assembly with same name is already loaded PowerShell/vscode-powershell#4727](https://github.com/PowerShell/vscode-powershell/issues/4727)

docs.title: 🧑‍💻 An Example Scenario
docs.text:
Numerous PowerShell modules include a dependency on the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) for authenticating to Microsoft’s online services. The latest version of the MSAL is actively maintained in AzureAD/microsoft-authentication-library-for-dotnet. However, modules that depend on the MSAL libraries (such as Microsoft.Identity.Client.dll) all update their releases with different versions of MSAL on different schedules. This results in version conflicts that break authentication flows whenever you try to use multiple modules in one session. Examples of modules that can be affected by this include:

  • Az.Accounts
  • ExchangeOnlineManagement
  • Microsoft.Graph.Authentication
  • MicrosoftTeams

…and many more.

You can manually attempt to work around this by checking which version of the conflicting DLLs are used by each of your PowerShell modules and then connect first to whichever service module uses the newest version of the DLL.

This works because of the “first one wins” rule and because the MSAL is designed to be backwards compatible.

DLL Pickle handles this for you by automatically releasing a new version of DLL Pickle whenever a new release of the MSAL is found. As long as you keep the DLL Pickle module up to date and load it first in your PowerShell profile (or manually load it first in your session), then all other PowerShell modules should be able to use this new MSAL that is loaded by DLL Pickle.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Any of the following PowerShell editions:

  • PowerShell on Linux, macOS, or Windows
  • Windows PowerShell 5.1

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Install-PSResource -Name DLLPickle -Prerelease

docs.title: 🥒 Using DLL Pickle
docs.text:
The easiest way to benefit from DLL Pickle is to import the module in your PowerShell profile before any other module or assembly is loaded. Just add the line Import-Module DLLPickle to your profile, save it, and start a new instance of PowerShell.

Alternatively, if you are starting work in a new PowerShell session in which you know you will be authenticating to multiple online services, you can run Import-Module DLLPickle at the beginning of your session and then proceed with the rest of your modules.

docs.title: Versioning
docs.text: This project follows the semantic versioning model. It also packages numerous dependencies that follow their own versioning. To maintain clarity, this project will follow SemVer standards and the following logic for version changes:

docs.title: Major Versions
docs.text: 🏷️ 2.x.x.x - The major version will only change if there is a breaking change in the DLL Pickle project.

docs.title: Minor Versions
docs.text:
🏷️ X.1.X.X - The minor version will change if any of the following occur:

  • New features are added to the project
  • New tracked assemblies are added to the project

docs.title: Build Versions
docs.text:
🏷️ X.X.1.X - The build version will change if any of the following occur:

  • Minor refactoring for performance, error handling, or logging
  • A new version of any packaged assembly is automatically updated within the project

docs.title: Revisions
docs.text:
🏷️ X.X.X.1234 - The revision segment of the version may change if any of the following occur:

  • Minor fixes for typos or formatting
  • Changes to documentation

docs.title: External Documentation
docs.text:
All libraries tracked for pre-loading by DLL Pickle are maintained and documented by their own code owners. Please see each project accordingly:

  • Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions
  • Microsoft.Identity.Client
  • Microsoft.Identity.Client.Broker
  • Microsoft.Identity.Client.Extensions.Msal
  • Microsoft.Identity.Client.NativeInterop
  • Microsoft.IdentityModel.Abstractions
  • Microsoft.IdentityModel.JsonWebTokens
  • Microsoft.IdentityModel.Logging
  • Microsoft.IdentityModel.Tokens
  • System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt

docs.title: Deep Dive
docs.text: I hope to put this into my own words as I have more time, but for now, here is a podcast-style audio deep dive and a video that explains how DLL Pickle works.

docs.title: Audio Discussion
docs.text: Ending PowerShell DLL Hell: How a Community Fix Uses “First-One-Wins” to Master Azure, Exchange, and Teams Connection Conflicts. An interactive audio deep-dive generated by NotebookLM.

docs.title: Video Explanation
docs.text: A look into a new PowerShell module called DLL Pickle, created to resolve common dependency conflicts experienced when managing multiple Microsoft services. This conflict, known as “DLL Hell” or the “DLL pickle,” occurs because different modules, such as Az.Accounts and ExchangeOnlineManagement, rely on varying versions of the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) and share the “.NET runtime’s ‘first-one-wins’ loading behavior in a single session. The DLL Pickle module functions as a proactive dependency pre-loader by intentionally loading the newest, backward-compatible version of the MSAL DLLs first. By using this technique, which is described as programmatic assembly resolution, the module ensures that all subsequent services use the compatible, latest version of the authentication libraries, preventing session failures caused by incompatible older files. Generated by NotebookLM.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The unified release workflow (release.yml) consolidates three competing release workflows into a single, coherent system that handles all release scenarios from a single source of truth.

docs.title: What It Replaces
docs.text:
• ❌ 2 - Release.yml (dependency-triggered releases) → CONSOLIDATED
  • ❌ 3 - Create Release.yml (code commit releases) → CONSOLIDATED
  • ❌ 4 - Publish Module.yml (standalone publish) → CONSOLIDATED

docs.title: What It Retains
docs.text: • ✅ 1 - Update Dependencies.yml (checks for updates, creates PRs) → UNCHANGED - This workflow still exists to create dependency update PRs. When merged to main, it triggers the new unified release workflow.

docs.title: Trigger Events
docs.text: The unified workflow is triggered in these scenarios:

docs.title: 1. Code Commit Trigger (Push to main)
docs.text:
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'src/DLLPickle/**'  # Only release if module code changes
      - '!**.md'             # Ignore documentation changes

Version Bump Logic: Automatic analysis of conventional commits since last release tag

  • BREAKING: → Major bump (e.g., 1.0.0 → 2.0.0)
  • feat: → Minor bump (e.g., 1.0.0 → 1.1.0)
  • fix:, chore:, etc. → Patch bump (e.g., 1.0.0 → 1.0.1)

Approval: No additional approval needed (code review via PR)

docs.title: 2. Dependency Update Trigger (Dependency workflow merges PR to main)
docs.text:
When 1 - Update Dependencies.yml auto-merges a PR with package updates, the commit push triggers this workflow via path-based activation.

Version Bump Logic: Either:

  • Conventional commits if there are other code commits in the push
  • Patch bump if only the dependency commit is new (default behavior)

Approval: No additional approval needed (dependencies were pre-approved)

docs.title: 3. Manual Dispatch Trigger (Explicit release request)
docs.text:
workflow_dispatch:
  inputs:
    version_bump:
      description: 'Version bump type (major, minor, patch) - overrides conventional commit analysis'
      required: false
      type: choice
      options: [auto, major, minor, patch]

Usage: Go to Actions → Release and Publish → Run workflow and select bump type

Version Bump Logic:

  • auto → Uses conventional commit analysis (like code trigger)
  • major, minor, patch → Uses explicitly specified bump

Approval: Requires approval if in psgallery environment (see below)

docs.title: Workflow Jobs (Sequential)
docs.text: The workflow is organized into 6 sequential jobs, each with clear responsibility:

docs.title: 1. analyze
docs.text:
Purpose: Determine if a release is needed and what version to release

Triggers:

  • Skips if commit contains [skip-release] or [no-release]
  • Always runs for code commits and manual dispatch

Outputs:

  • should_release - Boolean: whether to proceed with release
  • version_bump - String: major, minor, or patch
  • current_version - String: e.g., “1.2.3”
  • new_version - String: e.g., “1.2.4”
  • trigger_source - String: push, manual, or dependency

Uses Helper Script:

  • Get-VersionBump.ps1 - Analyzes git commit history since last tag
  • Parses conventional commit messages
  • Returns semantic version bump recommendation
  • Returns false if no release-worthy commits found

Key Logic:

# For push/code triggers: Analyze commits
$versionResult = & .\.github\scripts\Get-VersionBump.ps1

# For manual dispatch: Use input or analyze commits
if ($triggerSource -eq "manual" -and $versionBump -ne "auto") {
    # Use explicit bump from input
} else {
    # Use semantic analysis from commits
}

docs.title: 2. update-version
docs.text:
Depends on: analyze (only runs if should_release == true)

Purpose: Update the PowerShell module manifest with the new version and create a git commit + tag

Steps:

  1. Calls Update-ModuleVersion.ps1 to bump version in DLLPickle.psd1
  2. Updates ModuleVersion field
  3. Validates change was applied

  4. Returns success/error status

  5. Commits the version change to main

git commit -m "chore(release): bump version to X.Y.Z"
  1. Creates an annotated git tag
git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "Release X.Y.Z"
git push origin vX.Y.Z

Why This Order:

  • Updates manifest BEFORE building ensures build picks up new version
  • Creates tag BEFORE release ensures GitHub knows which commit to release
  • All done on main branch so dependencies resolve correctly

docs.title: 3. build-and-test
docs.text:
Depends on: update-version

Purpose: Build the module and run all tests to validate the release

Steps:

  1. Checkout updated repository (includes new tag)
  2. Build module using Invoke-Build
  3. Run all unit and integration tests
  4. Upload build artifacts if tests pass

Why This Order:

  • Ensures version is already bumped in manifest
  • Ensures tests run against actual release version
  • Prevents publishing if tests fail

If Tests Fail:

  • Job fails
  • GitHub release is NOT created
  • Version was already bumped and tagged (manual cleanup needed)

docs.title: 4. create-release
docs.text:
Depends on: build-and-test

Purpose: Create a GitHub release with generated release notes

Checks:

  1. Verifies release doesn’t already exist for this version
  2. Generates release notes from git history
  3. Creates release with notes

Release Notes Include:

  • Version number
  • Version bump type (major/minor/patch)
  • Trigger source (push/manual/dependency)
  • Recent commit messages
  • Links to Gallery and GitHub release page

Why This Order:

  • Ensures build is successful before creating release
  • Release marks the “official” release point for users

docs.title: 5. publish
docs.text:
Depends on: create-release

Approval Gate:

  • Uses GitHub environment: psgallery with required reviewers
  • Configure in repository settings: Settings → Environments → psgallery → Required reviewers
  • Prevents accidental publishes to live gallery

Purpose: Publish the module to PowerShell Gallery

Checks:

  1. Verifies manifest version matches expected version
  2. Checks if version already published (idempotency)
  3. Calls Publish-ToGallery.ps1 with retry logic
  4. Attempts up to 3 times with exponential backoff
  5. Waits for indexing (30 seconds default)
  6. Verifies publish via Find-Module

Requires Secret:

  • PSGALLERY_API_KEY - PowerShell Gallery API token
  • Store in repository secrets: Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions

Why This Order:

  • Ensures everything is tested and released before publishing
  • Uses approval gate to prevent accidents
  • Uses retry logic to handle transient API issues

docs.title: 6. summary
docs.text:
Depends on: All previous jobs (runs even if previous jobs fail)

Purpose: Generate workflow summary with status and links

Output:

  • Displays in workflow run summary
  • Shows final status of all jobs
  • Provides links to release, gallery, and workflow run

docs.title: Decision Tree
docs.text:
This diagram shows how the workflow determines whether to release and what version to bump:

┌─ Push to main (with code changes)
│  ├─ Analyze commits since last tag
│  ├─ If conventional commits found → Determine bump (major/minor/patch)
│  └─ Release with semantic version
│
├─ Dependency workflow merges PR to main
│  ├─ Push event triggers workflow
│  ├─ Analyze commits (likely just dep update commit)
│  ├─ Likely patch bump (or based on actual commits)
│  └─ Release with bump version
│
└─ Manual dispatch with version_bump
   ├─ If "auto" → Analyze commits (same as push)
   ├─ If "major/minor/patch" → Use explicit bump
   └─ Release with specified version

docs.title: Repository Secrets
docs.text:
Required:

PSGALLERY_API_KEY=<your-powershell-gallery-api-token>

Get this from PowerShell Gallery account settings: https://www.powershellgallery.com/account/apikeys

docs.title: Environment Approval Gate
docs.text:
Configure approval for PSGallery publishes:

  1. Go to Settings → Environments
  2. Click New environment → Name it psgallery
  3. Check Required reviewers
  4. Add team members who can approve releases

docs.title: Path-Based Triggering
docs.text:
The workflow only triggers on changes to specific paths:

  • ✅ Changes in src/DLLPickle/ → TRIGGER
  • ❌ Changes only in docs → SKIP
  • ❌ Changes only in markdown → SKIP

This prevents unnecessary releases for documentation changes.

docs.title: 1. Automatic Release (Code Commit)
docs.text:
# 1. Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feat/new-feature

# 2. Make code changes
echo "New feature code" >> src/DLLPickle/Public/Get-Example.ps1

# 3. Commit with conventional message
git commit -m "feat: add new Get-Example function"

# 4. Create PR, get review, merge to main
git push origin feat/new-feature
# ... Create PR and merge ...

# Result: Workflow triggers automatically
# - Analyzes commit: "feat: add..." → minor bump
# - Updates version: 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
# - Builds and tests
# - Creates release v1.1.0
# - Publishes to gallery (after approval)

docs.title: 2. Dependency Update Release
docs.text:
# 1. Dependency workflow runs on schedule (2 AM UTC daily)
# 2. Finds updates to packages in Packages.json
# 3. Creates/updates PR with updates
# 4. Auto-merges PR when checks pass

# Result: Commit push to main triggers release workflow
# - Detects commit from dependency update
# - Patches version (e.g., 1.1.0 → 1.1.1)
# - Builds and tests
# - Creates release v1.1.1
# - Publishes to gallery

docs.title: 3. Manual Release (Emergency Hotfix)
docs.text:
# 1. Go to GitHub repository
# 2. Actions → Release and Publish → Run workflow
# 3. Select version_bump: "patch"
# 4. Click "Run workflow"

# Result: Workflow runs immediately
# - Uses explicit patch bump
# - Updated version: 1.1.1 → 1.1.2
# - Builds and tests
# - Creates release v1.1.2
# - Publishes to gallery (after approval)

docs.title: 4. Skip Release on Specific Commit
docs.text:
# Skip release for this commit
git commit -m "chore: update readme [skip-release]"

# Or
git commit -m "docs: update docs [no-release]"

# Result: Workflow runs but analyze job exits early
# - No release created
# - Useful for documentation-only or chore commits

docs.title: If Build Fails
docs.text:
What happens:

  • build-and-test job fails
  • create-release and publish jobs are skipped
  • Version was already bumped and tagged (on main branch)

Recovery:

  1. Fix the issue locally
  2. Create new PR with fix
  3. Merge to main
  4. New release will be created for the fix commit

Note: Manual cleanup may be needed if the failed version tag should be removed

docs.title: If Publish Fails
docs.text:
What happens:

  • publish job fails (usually after 3 retry attempts)
  • GitHub release exists but module not in gallery
  • Version tag exists on main

Recovery:

  1. Fix the underlying issue (e.g., API key, network)
  2. Either:
  3. Manually run Publish-ToGallery.ps1 locally, OR
  4. Manually trigger workflow with workflow_dispatch and “auto” option

docs.title: If Approval Gate Times Out
docs.text:
What happens:

  • publish job waits for approval
  • If approval not provided within environment timeout (GitHub default: unlimited)
  • Job stays pending

Recovery:

  1. Visit workflow run page
  2. Click “Review deployments”
  3. Select “Approve” or “Reject”

docs.title: Removing Old Workflows
docs.text:
Once the unified workflow is tested and validated, remove these legacy workflows:

  1. Delete .github/workflows/2 - Release.yml
git rm '.github/workflows/2 - Release.yml'
git commit -m "chore: remove legacy release workflow (consolidated to release.yml)"
  1. Delete .github/workflows/4 - Publish Module.yml
git rm '.github/workflows/4 - Publish Module.yml'
git commit -m "chore: remove legacy publish workflow (consolidated to release.yml)"
  1. Keep .github/workflows/1 - Update Dependencies.yml
  2. This still creates dependency update PRs
  3. They merge to main, which triggers the unified release workflow

Do NOT delete until:

  • ✅ Unified workflow has been tested with at least one successful release
  • ✅ Both code commit and dependency update pathways have been validated
  • ✅ All team members understand the new system

docs.title: Helper Scripts Used
docs.text:
The unified workflow calls these PowerShell helper scripts (created in Phase 2):

Script Purpose Called By Get-VersionBump.ps1 Analyze commits, determine version bump analyze job Update-ModuleVersion.ps1 Update .psd1 manifest version update-version job Publish-ToGallery.ps1 Publish to gallery with retry logic publish job

See .github/scripts/README.md for detailed documentation on each script.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Key environment variables used:

MODULE_NAME = "DLLPickle"
MODULE_DIR = "./src/DLLPickle"
MANIFEST_PATH = "./src/DLLPickle/DLLPickle.psd1"

These are set at the workflow level and available to all jobs.

docs.title: Check Release Status
docs.text:
1. Go to GitHub repository → Actions
  2. Click Release and Publish workflow
  3. View recent runs
  4. Click a run to see detailed job status

docs.title: Enable Notifications
docs.text:
1. Go to GitHub Settings → Notifications
  2. Configure email/web notifications for workflow completions

docs.title: Check Gallery Listing
docs.text:
After publish completes, verify module is on PSGallery:

Find-Module -Name DLLPickle -Repository PSGallery | Select-Object Version, PublishedDate

docs.title: “Version already published” Error
docs.text:
Problem: Workflow tries to publish version that already exists on gallery

Solution: Manual override in publish job check - modify already_published condition or increment version and re-run

docs.title: “Approval gate timeout”
docs.text:
Problem: publish job waits forever for approval that never comes

Solution:

  1. Visit workflow run page
  2. Click “Review deployments” button
  3. Approve or reject the deployment

docs.title: “Tag already exists”
docs.text:
Problem: Git tag for release version already exists

Solution: Manual cleanup - delete old tag with git tag -d vX.Y.Z && git push origin :refs/tags/vX.Y.Z

docs.title: “Manifest version doesn’t match”
docs.text:
Problem: Published module version doesn’t match expected version

Solution: Check manifest file directly - run Update-ModuleVersion.ps1 locally to verify

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. ✅ Review this unified workflow - Understand how it works
  2. 📝 Test with a code commit - Make a small code change, merge to main, verify release is created
  3. 📝 Test with dependency update - Wait for dependency workflow or manually trigger, verify release
  4. 📝 Configure approvals - Set up psgallery environment with required reviewers
  5. 🗑️ Remove legacy workflows - Delete 2 - Release.yml and 4 - Publish Module.yml after validation
  6. 📚 Update documentation - Update team wiki/docs to reference new unified workflow

```mermaid flowchart TD Start([Publish Module to PowerShell Gallery]) → Trigger{Trigger Type}

Trigger -->|Release Published| TriggerA[Release Event]
Trigger -->|Manual Dispatch| TriggerB[Workflow Dispatch]

TriggerA --> Harden[Harden Runner - Audit Egress]
TriggerB --> Harden

Harden --> Checkout[Checkout Repository Code]
Checkout --> Setup[Setup PowerShell Environment]
Setup --> Validate[Validate Manifest & Version]

Validate --> CheckVersion{Version Check}
CheckVersion -->|Version Exists in Gallery| WarnContinue["⚠️ Warning: Version exists<br/>Continue anyway"]
CheckVersion -->|Version Not Found| ProceedOK["✓ Version available"]

WarnContinue --> VersionAlign{Version Alignment Check}
ProceedOK --> VersionAlign

VersionAlign -->|Mismatch| WarnMismatch["⚠️ Warning: Version mismatch<br/>between tag/release/manifest"]
VersionAlign -->|Aligned| VersionOK["✓ Versions aligned"]

WarnMismatch --> SetOutputs[Set Workflow Outputs:<br/>name, baseVersion, fullVersion,<br/>isPrerelease, releaseTag]
VersionOK --> SetOutputs

SetOutputs --> InstallDeps[Install PowerShellGet Dependencies]
InstallDeps --> Publish[Publish Module to PSGallery]

Publish --> RetryLogic{Publish Attempt}
RetryLogic -->|Retry 1| Wait1[Wait 5 seconds]
RetryLogic -->|Retry 2| Wait2[Wait 10 seconds]
RetryLogic -->|Retry 3| Wait3[Wait 15 seconds]

Wait1 --> Publish
Wait2 --> Publish
Wait3 --> Publish

RetryLogic -->|Success| PublishOK["✓ Module Published"]
RetryLogic -->|All Failed| PublishFail["❌ Publish Failed<br/>Exit 1"]

PublishOK --> Wait30[Wait 30 seconds for indexing]
Wait30 --> VerifyPublish[Verify Module in Gallery]

VerifyPublish --> ArtifactPrep[Prepare Artifact:<br/>Copy module files,<br/>Generate publish-info.json,<br/>Calculate SHA256 file hashes]

ArtifactPrep --> UploadArtifact[Upload Module Artifact<br/>Retention: 30 days]
UploadArtifact --> Summary[Create Workflow Summary]

Summary --> CheckRelease[Check for Existing<br/>GitHub Release]

CheckRelease --> ReleaseExists{Release Exists?}
ReleaseExists -->|Yes| Skip["✓ Release exists<br/>Skip creation"]
ReleaseExists -->|No| CreateRelease[Create GitHub Release]

CreateRelease --> Prerelease{Is Prerelease?}
Prerelease -->|Yes| CreatePre[Create as Prerelease<br/>with tag v{version}]
Prerelease -->|No| CreateStable[Create as Stable Release<br/>with tag v{version}]

CreatePre --> ReleaseBody[Generate Release Body:<br/>Installation instructions,<br/>Module info, Links]
CreateStable --> ReleaseBody

ReleaseBody --> ReleaseSuccess["✓ GitHub Release Created"]

Skip --> End([Workflow Complete])
ReleaseSuccess --> End
PublishFail --> End

style Start fill:#e1f5e1
style End fill:#e1f5e1
style PublishOK fill:#d4edda
style ReleaseSuccess fill:#d4edda
style PublishFail fill:#f8d7da
style WarnContinue fill:#fff3cd
style WarnMismatch fill:#fff3cd
style Prerelease fill:#cfe2ff
style CheckVersion fill:#cfe2ff
style VersionAlign fill:#cfe2ff
style ReleaseExists fill:#cfe2ff

```

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The DLLPickle repository uses a two-stage workflow architecture for automated dependency management:

  1. Update Dependencies - Creates PRs with dependency updates
  2. Release and Publish - Bumps version, creates releases, and publishes to PSGallery

```mermaid flowchart TD A[Scheduled Dependency Check] → | Get Dependency Updates | DependencyUpdates B[Push to Main Branch] → | Push Module Code Changes | PushCommit

DependencyUpdates{Were dependencies updated?}
DependencyUpdates --> | Yes | BumpVersionDependencies("Bump Version<br />(Patch)")
DependencyUpdates --> | No | EndWorkflow("End Dependency<br />Workflow")
BumpVersionDependencies --> IsNewVersion{Is New Version?}
IsNewVersion --> | No | CreateIssue(Create Issue)
IsNewVersion --> | Yes | CreateRelease

PushCommit{Check commit message for version trigger.}
PushCommit --> |"No: Chore, Docs, other" | EndPush(End Code<br />Workflow)
PushCommit --> |"Yes: Feat, Fix, Minor, Major, Refactor, Breaking" | BumpVersionPush("Bump Version per Commit Message Type")
BumpVersionPush --> ReleaseTrigger{Release Workflow Trigger}
ReleaseTrigger --> | Automatic | EndPushWorkflow["End Code<br />Workflow"]
ReleaseTrigger --> | Manual | CreateRelease

CreateRelease("Create GitHub<br />Release") --> PublishModule(Publish to<br />PSGallery)

```

docs.title: Workflow 1: Update Dependencies
docs.text:
File: .github/workflows/Update Dependencies.yml

Trigger: Daily at 2 AM UTC (scheduled) or manual

Purpose: Automatically check for NuGet package updates and create PRs

docs.title: Process Flow
docs.text:
1. Check for Updates - Queries NuGet API for latest versions
  2. Download & Extract - Downloads new packages if updates are available
  3. Create/Update Branch - Creates or updates chore/update-packages branch
  4. Create/Update PR - Opens PR with dependency changes
  5. Request Reviews - Adds labels and requests Copilot review
  6. Wait for Checks - Waits up to 30 minutes for CI checks
  7. Auto-Approve - Approves PR automatically if all checks pass

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Concurrency Control - Prevents overlapping runs
  • ✅ Fork Safety - Only runs on main repository
  • ✅ Idempotent - Updates existing PRs instead of creating duplicates
  • ✅ Smart Waiting - Waits for CodeQL and other checks
  • ✅ Auto-Approval - Approves after successful checks

docs.title: Workflow 2: Release and Publish
docs.text:
File: .github/workflows/Release and Publish.yml

Trigger: PR merge to main with dependencies label, or manual

Purpose: Version bump, create GitHub releases, and publish to PSGallery

docs.title: Process Flow (Update Dependencies)
docs.text:
1. Determine Bump Type - Patch (default), minor, or major
  2. Update Module Version - Updates DLLPickle.psd1
  3. Extract Changes - Captures PR summary for release notes
  4. Commit Version - Commits version bump to main
  5. Create Release - Creates tagged GitHub release
  6. Publish Module - Publishes to PowerShell Gallery

docs.title: Key Features (Update Dependencies)
docs.text:
• ✅ Automatic Trigger - Runs on dependency PR merge
  • ✅ Manual Trigger - Can be run manually with version control
  • ✅ Smart Versioning - Detects version bump type from PR
  • ✅ Rich Release Notes - Auto-generates from PR content
  • ✅ PSGallery Publishing - Automated module publication

docs.title: Version Bump Logic
docs.text:
• Patch (default): Dependency updates
  • Minor: Feature additions (detect via PR title keywords)
  • Major: Breaking changes (detect via PR title keywords)
  • Manual: Choose any bump type when manually triggered

docs.title: Complete Update Flow
docs.text: mermaid graph TD A[Scheduled: 2 AM UTC] --> B[Check NuGet Versions] B --> C{Updates Available?} C -->|No| D[Exit] C -->|Yes| E[Download Packages] E --> F[Create/Update PR] F --> G[Request Copilot Review] G --> H[Run CodeQL & Checks] H --> I{Checks Pass?} I -->|No| J[Manual Review Needed] I -->|Yes| K[Auto-Approve PR] K --> L[Human Merges PR] L --> M[Release Workflow Triggers] M --> N[Bump Module Version] N --> O[Create GitHub Release] O --> P[Publish to PSGallery] P --> Q[Complete]

docs.title: Update Dependencies Workflow
docs.text:
• Only runs on SamErde/DLLPickle repository (not forks)
  • Single concurrent run allowed (prevents conflicts)
  • Won’t approve if any checks fail
  • 30-minute timeout for check completion
  • Uses bot account for commits and PRs

docs.title: Release Workflow
docs.text:
• Only triggers on merged PRs with dependencies label
  • Validates module manifest before publishing
  • Creates git tags for version tracking
  • Can be manually triggered for non-automated releases
  • Fails fast if PSGallery publish errors occur

docs.title: Trigger Dependency Check
docs.text: gh workflow run "Update Dependencies"

docs.title: Trigger Release (with version control)
docs.text:
# Patch release (0.0.X)
gh workflow run "Release and Publish" -f version_bump=patch

# Minor release (0.X.0)
gh workflow run "Release and Publish" -f version_bump=minor

# Major release (X.0.0)
gh workflow run "Release and Publish" -f version_bump=major

docs.title: Enable Auto-Merge
docs.text:
To fully automate the process, enable auto-merge on the created PR:

gh pr merge <PR_NUMBER> --auto --squash

This allows PRs to merge automatically once approved and checks pass.

docs.title: PR Not Auto-Approved
docs.text:
• Check that all required checks passed
  • Verify PAT_CREATEPR has appropriate permissions
  • Review workflow logs for timeout issues

docs.title: Release Not Triggered
docs.text:
• Ensure merged PR has dependencies label
  • Verify PR was merged to main branch
  • Check that PR merge event triggered workflow

docs.title: PSGallery Publish Failed
docs.text:
• Validate PSGALLERY_API_KEY is set correctly
  • Ensure API key has publish permissions
  • Check module manifest is valid (Test-ModuleManifest)

docs.title: Detailed Workflow Diagrams
docs.text: This project relies on several workflows to keep library dependencies updated automatically.

docs.title: Update Monitored Packages
docs.text:
```mermaid flowchart

Start([Update Monitored Packages]) --> LoopStart[Initialize Loop: process each tracked library in Packages.json]

subgraph Left ["Check for Updates to Monitored Packages"]

    JSON["Sample entry in Packages.json:<br/><br/>{<br/>  name: Microsoft.Identity.Client.Broker,<br/>  version: 4.79.2<br/>}"]
    style JSON text-align: left
    JSON --> CompareVersion

    LoopStart[Initialize Loop: process each tracked library in Packages.json]
    LoopStart --> CheckPackage[Check NuGet.org for the latest package version]
    CheckPackage --> CompareVersion{Is JSON tracking the latest version number?}

    CompareVersion -->|No:<br />Current Entry < Latest Release | UpdateAvailable["Update Available<br/>Add to Summary"]
    CompareVersion -->|Yes:<br />Current Entry = Latest Release | UpToDate[✓ Already Up to Date]

    UpdateAvailable --> MarkUpdate[Set updates_available=true<br/>Append to update_summary]
    UpToDate --> NextPackage{More Packages?}
    MarkUpdate --> NextPackage

    NextPackage -->|Yes| CheckPackage
    NextPackage -->|No| AnyUpdates{Updates Available?}

end

subgraph Right ["Download Updates if Available"]

    AnyUpdates -->|No| EndNoUpdate["✓ All packages up to date<br/>Workflow Complete"]
    AnyUpdates -->|Yes| DownloadLoop

    DownloadLoop --> Download[Download .nupkg<br/>from NuGet.org]
    Download --> Extract[Extract nupkg<br/>as ZIP archive]

    Extract --> FindFramework[Search for DLLs in<br/>framework paths]

    FindFramework --> TryPaths{Try Framework Paths}
    TryPaths -->|netstandard2.0| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|netstandard2.1| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|net6.0| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|net472| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|runtimes/win paths| PathFound1

    PathFound1{DLLs Found?}
    PathFound1 -->|Yes| CopyDLLs[Copy DLLs to<br/>Assembly folder]
    PathFound1 -->|No| WarnNoDLL["⚠️ Warning: No compatible DLLs<br/>Continue to next"]

    CopyDLLs --> UpdateJSON[Update version in<br/>Packages.json]
    UpdateJSON --> MarkUpdated[Mark as updated]

    MarkUpdated --> MoreDownloads{More Packages?}
    WarnNoDLL --> MoreDownloads

    MoreDownloads -->|Yes| DownloadLoop
    MoreDownloads -->|No| SaveJSON[Save updated<br/>Packages.json]

end

subgraph Finalization [“Finalize and Release”]

SaveJSON --> ModuleManifest[Read Module Manifest]

ModuleManifest --> IncrementVersion[Increment Patch Version<br/>x.y.z → x.y.z+1]
IncrementVersion --> UpdateManifest[Update Module Manifest<br/>with new version]

UpdateManifest --> GitConfig[Configure Git User:<br/>github-actions bot]

GitConfig --> GitStage[Stage Changes:<br/>Assembly/*.dll<br/>Assembly/Packages.json<br/>DLLPickle.psd1]

GitStage --> GitCommit[Commit Changes:<br/>Update MSAL packages:<br/>summary message]
GitCommit --> GitPush[Push to Repository]

GitPush --> CreateRelease[Create GitHub Release]
CreateRelease --> ReleaseDetails[Set Release Details:<br/>Tag: v{new_version}<br/>Title: Release v{new_version}<br/>Body: Package update summary]

ReleaseDetails --> PublishRelease[Publish Release:<br/>Not draft, Not prerelease]

PublishRelease --> ReleaseSuccess["✓ GitHub Release Created"]

end

```

```mermaid flowchart

Start([Update Monitored Packages]) --> LoopStart[Initialize Loop: process each tracked library in Packages.json]

subgraph Left ["Check for Updates to Monitored Packages"]

    JSON["Sample entry in Packages.json:<br/><br/>{<br/>  name: Microsoft.Identity.Client.Broker,<br/>  version: 4.79.2<br/>}"]
    style JSON text-align: left
    JSON --> CompareVersion

    LoopStart[Initialize Loop: process each tracked library in Packages.json]
    LoopStart --> CheckPackage[Check NuGet.org for the latest package version]
    CheckPackage --> CompareVersion{Is JSON tracking the latest version number?}

    CompareVersion -->|No:<br />Current Entry < Latest Release | UpdateAvailable["Update Available<br/>Add to Summary"]
    CompareVersion -->|Yes:<br />Current Entry = Latest Release | UpToDate[✓ Already Up to Date]

    UpdateAvailable --> MarkUpdate[Set updates_available=true<br/>Append to update_summary]
    UpToDate --> NextPackage{More Packages?}
    MarkUpdate --> NextPackage

    NextPackage -->|Yes| CheckPackage
    NextPackage -->|No| AnyUpdates{Updates Available?}

end

subgraph Right ["Download Updates if Available"]

    AnyUpdates -->|No| EndNoUpdate["✓ All packages up to date<br/>Workflow Complete"]
    AnyUpdates -->|Yes| DownloadLoop

    DownloadLoop --> Download[Download .nupkg<br/>from NuGet.org]
    Download --> Extract[Extract nupkg<br/>as ZIP archive]

    Extract --> FindFramework[Search for DLLs in<br/>framework paths]

    FindFramework --> TryPaths{Try Framework Paths}
    TryPaths -->|netstandard2.0| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|netstandard2.1| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|net6.0| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|net472| PathFound1
    TryPaths -->|runtimes/win paths| PathFound1

    PathFound1{DLLs Found?}
    PathFound1 -->|Yes| CopyDLLs[Copy DLLs to<br/>Assembly folder]
    PathFound1 -->|No| WarnNoDLL["⚠️ Warning: No compatible DLLs<br/>Continue to next"]

    CopyDLLs --> UpdateJSON[Update version in<br/>Packages.json]
    UpdateJSON --> MarkUpdated[Mark as updated]

    MarkUpdated --> MoreDownloads{More Packages?}
    WarnNoDLL --> MoreDownloads

    MoreDownloads -->|Yes| DownloadLoop
    MoreDownloads -->|No| SaveJSON[Save updated<br/>Packages.json]

end

subgraph Finalization [“Finalize and Release”]

SaveJSON --> ModuleManifest[Read Module Manifest]

ModuleManifest --> IncrementVersion[Increment Patch Version<br/>x.y.z → x.y.z+1]
IncrementVersion --> UpdateManifest[Update Module Manifest<br/>with new version]

UpdateManifest --> GitConfig[Configure Git User:<br/>github-actions bot]

GitConfig --> GitStage[Stage Changes:<br/>Assembly/*.dll<br/>Assembly/Packages.json<br/>DLLPickle.psd1]

GitStage --> GitCommit[Commit Changes:<br/>Update MSAL packages:<br/>summary message]
GitCommit --> GitPush[Push to Repository]

GitPush --> CreateRelease[Create GitHub Release]
CreateRelease --> ReleaseDetails[Set Release Details:<br/>Tag: v{new_version}<br/>Title: Release v{new_version}<br/>Body: Package update summary]

ReleaseDetails --> PublishRelease[Publish Release:<br/>Not draft, Not prerelease]

PublishRelease --> ReleaseSuccess["✓ GitHub Release Created"]

ReleaseSuccess --> EndSuccess([Workflow Complete])
EndNoUpdate --> EndSuccess
Error1 --> EndSuccess

end ```

docs.title: Workflow Design Principles
docs.text:
• Require a PR for any changes to files/folders under the source directory (./src/DLLPickle/*).
  • Follow Conventional Commits guidelines for every commit message.
  • Do not manually increment versions in the PowerShell module manifest (./src/DLLPickle/DLLPickle.psd1).

docs.title: Concurrency and race-proofing
docs.text:
• Concurrency: Add concurrency keys per workflow (e.g., update-deps, release) to prevent overlapping runs and double-bumps when two PRs merge close together.
  • Single updater PR: Ensure the daily job reuses a fixed branch (e.g., chore/update-packages) and force-push/rebase it; auto-close older PRs if any exist.

docs.title: Avoiding workflow loops
docs.text:
• Skip tokens: Use a standard token such as [skip-build] or [no-release] in commit messages and conditional if: guards in jobs to prevent the version-bump commit from re-triggering the release/publish steps.
  • Path filters: Keep strong paths includes/excludes to avoid artifact/doc-only pushes from triggering build/release.

docs.title: Versioning strategy (pick ONE)
docs.text:
• Tag-driven: Create a tag (vX.Y.Z) derived from conventional commits at merge time, then build uses the tag to set the ModuleVersion without committing the .psd1 bump to main. This is clean and avoids a bump commit loop.
  • PR bump: Have a bot open a short PR with the .psd1 bump before merging to main. This keeps main source-of-truth and avoids post-merge commits to main.

docs.title: PR pipeline hygiene
docs.text:
• Labels by path: Auto-label PRs based on changed paths (src/docs/workflows) to route checks/review.
  • Draft until ready: Let the daily updater open PRs as draft until tests pass.
  • Auto-merge rules: Optionally enable auto-merge for the updater PR when all checks pass.

docs.title: (a) Scheduled Workflow: Update Monitored Packages
docs.text:
• Check nuget.org for new versions of tracked DLLs in Packages.json
  • Update DLLs in ./src/DLLPickle/Lib

docs.title: (b) Work: Code Contributions
docs.text: Contribution Type Path Requirements Source Code: features, fixes, quality improvements ./src Pull Request Documentation: additions, fixes, improvements ./docs Repository: workflows, repository settings, metadata Other

docs.title: 2. Pull Requests
docs.text:
Actions may be triggered when pull requests are submitted or merged to specific branches (or all branches).

Trigger Branch Details PR: On Submit all PR: On Merge main

(placeholder)

docs.title: 3. Commits
docs.text: Actions may be triggered when a commit is pushed to any particular branch or path. (Triggered by merging a PR or by directly commiting to a branch.)

docs.title: On push to main
docs.text: Condition Action all Run code quality and security checks if path in: ./src/**, !./src/archive/**,! ./src/artifacts/**and flag not DoNotReprocessPush Trigger Build, Release, Publish WorkflowsIncrement version based on conventional commitsUpdate module manifest version with a commit message flag to prevent loopRun Build Module workflow

docs.title: 4: Workflow: Build, Release, Publish
docs.text:
This workflow (or series of workflows) will be triggered by the previous step that processes commits to the source if/after those commits pass required checks.

If any of these steps fail, the workflow should exit with a warning and detailed summary. (It may be helpful to provide a way to manually trigger any one of these steps during inevitable troubleshooting of this overarching workflow.)

  • Build Module
  • Check if a GitHub release and PSGallery release already exists with this version
  • Update changelog with details from commit messages
  • Create GitHub release with details from changelog as notes
  • Publish to PowerShell Gallery
  • Provide a summary

docs.title: Description
docs.text: A PowerShell module that helps you get un-stuck from version conflicts that occur when using modules that depend on different versions of the same dependency (such as the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL)).

docs.title: Get-DLLsInModulePath
docs.text: Show a list of all DLLs in PowerShell module paths that contain the specified product name in their FileInfo property.

docs.title: Get-ModulesWithDependency
docs.text: Finds installed PowerShell modules that have a common file dependency.

docs.title: Get-ModulesWithVersionSortedIdentityClient
docs.text: Get a list of modules with the MSAL, and which versions each have packaged.

docs.title: Import-DPLibrary
docs.text: Import DLLPickle libraries based on Packages.json configuration.

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Show a list of all DLLs in PowerShell module paths that contain the specified product name in their FileInfo property.

docs.title: __AllParameterSets
docs.text:
Find-DLLInPSModulePath [[-ProductName] <string>] [[-FileName] <string>] [[-Path] <string[]>]
 [[-ExcludeDirectories] <string[]>] [[-Scope] <string>] [-NewestVersion] [-ShowDetails]
 [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: ALIASES
docs.text: This cmdlet has the following aliases, {{Insert list of aliases}}

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: Check all installed PowerShell module locations for DLL files that have the specified product name (e.g., ‘Microsoft Identity’) in their file’s ProductName attribute. By default, searches all paths in the PSModulePath environment variable. Can optionally check custom locations using the -Path parameter.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
Find-DLLInPSModulePath -ProductName “Microsoft Identity”

Find all DLLs with ‘Microsoft Identity’ in their ProductName property within installed PowerShell module locations.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text:
Find-DLLInPSModulePath -FileName “Microsoft.IdentityModel*.dll”

Find all DLL files matching the pattern ‘Microsoft.IdentityModel*.dll’ that also have ‘Microsoft Identity’ in their ProductName.

Example Output:

InternalName ProductVersion Module ------------ -------------- ------ Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.dll 9.5.0.0 DLLPickle Microsoft.IdentityModel.Abstractions.dll 0.0.0.0 Az.Accounts Microsoft.IdentityModel.JsonWebTokens.dll 8.6.0.0 ExchangeOnlineManagement Microsoft.IdentityModel.Logging.dll 8.6.0.0 ExchangeOnlineManagement Microsoft.IdentityModel.Protocols.dll 8.6.1.0 WinTuner Microsoft.IdentityModel.Protocols.OpenIdConnect.dll 8.6.1.0 WinTuner Microsoft.IdentityModel.Tokens.dll 8.6.0.0 ExchangeOnlineManagement Microsoft.IdentityModel.Validators.dll 8.6.1.0 WinTuner System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt.dll 8.6.0.0 ExchangeOnlineManagement

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Reports the version, path, and scope that would be imported for a given module name.

docs.title: __AllParameterSets

docs.title: ALIASES
docs.text: This cmdlet has the following aliases, {{Insert list of aliases}}

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: Searches PSModulePath in order to determine which version of a module would be imported by Import-Module, mimicking PowerShell’s module resolution logic.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text: Get-ModuleImportCandidate -Name “Pester” Gets the effective module information for Pester from any scope.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text: Get-ModuleImportCandidate “PSReadLine” -Scope CurrentUser Gets the effective module information for PSReadLine only from the CurrentUser scope.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 3
docs.text: “Pester”, “PSReadLine” | Get-ModuleImportCandidate Gets the effective module information for multiple modules from any scope.

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Evaluates the import order of specified modules based on their versions and the location in PSModulePath.

docs.title: SYNTAX
docs.text: Get-ModuleImportOrder [[-Name] <String[]>] [-ProgressAction <ActionPreference>] [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: This function evaluates the import order of specified modules based on their versions and the location in PSModulePath. It uses Get-ModuleImportCandidate to determine which version of each module would be imported by Import-Module, and then sorts them by the version of ‘Microsoft.Identity.Client.dll’ that is packaged with each module.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
Get-ModuleImportOrder -Name 'Az.Accounts','ExchangeOnlineManagement'

Returns a list of modules ordered by the version of ‘Microsoft.Identity.Client.dll’ they contain.

docs.title: -Name
docs.text:
A list of module names to evaluate for proper import order. Wildcards are allowed.

Type: String[]
Parameter Sets: (All)
Aliases:

Required: False
Position: 1
Default value: None
Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName)
Accept wildcard characters: False

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Finds installed PowerShell modules that have a common file dependency.

docs.title: __AllParameterSets
docs.text: Get-ModulesWithDependency [-FileName] <string> [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: ALIASES
docs.text: This cmdlet has the following aliases, {{Insert list of aliases}}

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: This function queries installed PowerShell resources to identify all modules that have a common dependency on a specific file.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text:
Get-ModulesWithDependency -FileName ‘Microsoft.Identity.Client.dll’ | Format-Table Name,Version,@{N=’FileName’;E={($_.DependencyPath.Split(‘'))[-1]}},DependencyVersion

This will format the output to show the module name, version, file name, and dependency version in a table.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text:
Get-ModulesWithDependency -FileName ‘Microsoft.Identity.Client.dll’

This will return an array of PSResourceInfo objects for modules with the specified dependency.

docs.title: -FileName
docs.text:
The name of the file dependency to search for in the module’s manifest file list.

docs.title: SYNOPSIS
docs.text: Import DLLPickle libraries based on Packages.json configuration.

docs.title: __AllParameterSets
docs.text: Import-DPLibrary [-ImportAll] [<CommonParameters>]

docs.title: ALIASES
docs.text: This cmdlet has the following aliases, {{Insert list of aliases}}

docs.title: DESCRIPTION
docs.text: Import all DLLs (libraries) that are tracked and marked for auto-import in the Packages.json file.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 1
docs.text: Import-DPLibrary Imports all DLLPickle libraries marked for auto-import.

docs.title: EXAMPLE 2
docs.text: Import-DPLibrary -ImportAll Imports all DLLPickle libraries, ignoring auto-import settings.

docs.title: -ImportAll
docs.text:
Ignore preset ‘autoImport’ values and attempt to import all packages.
